title: Appropriation Act, 2025
text:
ACT
Supplement to the Sierra Leone Gazette Vol. CLXVI, No 3
dated 16th Ja nuary, 2025
SicneD this 8th day ef January, 2025
DR. JULIUS MAADA BIO,
President.
No. 2 2025
ae Bence
Sierra Leone
The Appropriation Act, 2625 Short tithe.
Being an Act to authorise expenditure from the Consolidated
Fund for the services of Sierra Leone for the year 2025 and for
other related matters.
Enactep by the President and Members of Parliament in this
present Parliament assembled.
E J Date of com-
mencement.
WHEREAS it is necessary to make provision for the issue from the
Consolidated Fund of sums of money necessary to meet the
expenditure to carry on the services of the Government.

2 No.2 Appropriation Act 2025
Commencement. 1. This Act shall be deemed to have come into operation on
the ist day of January, 2025.
NLe 2. The sum of twenty seven billion seven hundred and sixteen
27,716,357,400 million three hundred and fifty seven thousand four hundred Leones
granted for
shall be and is hereby granted to His Excellency the President for and
2025.
during the year two thousand and twenty five to be applied and
expended in the manner described and for the several services set
forth in the Schedule.
NLe 3. Thesum of twenty seven billion seven hundred and sixteen
27,716,357,400 million three hundred and fifty seven thousand four hundred Leones
Charged on
referred to in section 2, shall be and is hereby declared to be charged
revenue.
upon and made payable from and out of the Consolidated Fund for
the year two thousand and twenty five and the payment thereof
shall be taken to begin on and immediately after the first day of
January, two thousand and twenty five.
Accountant 4, The Accountant-General is hereby authorised and required
General's to pay out of the Consolidated Fund for the several services specified
authority for
in the Schedule, such sums as may be specified in the Warrant or
payment.
Order of the Minister of Finance, issued from time to time up to the
sum of twenty seven billion seven hundred and sixteen million three
hundred and fifty seven thousand four hundred Leones which shall
come in course of payment during the year ending on the thirty-first
day of December, two thousand and twenty five.
Excess of 5. The amount from time to time paid for and in respect of the
Expenditure. expenditure authorised in section 4 shall not exceed in the gross the
sum of twenty seven billion seven hundred and sixteen million three
hundred and fifty seven thousand four hundred Leones from the
first day of January, two thousand and twenty five inclusive, and
any balances remaining unissued at the end of December, two
thousand and twenty five shall lapse and not be available for making
any payment in the following year.

8 No.2 Appropriation Act 2025
Nate }. The amounts for Charged Emoluments, Gratuities and Other Retirement
Benefits, and Contributions to Social Security are included in the provision
for Salaries and Wages.
Note 2. The Amount provided for Salaries and Wages is the provision for the Financial
Year 2025 for all Ministries, Departments and Agencies, including
Government's contribution to Social Security as Employer to the Social
Security Scheme.
Note 3. Contingency Fund (Allocated) which is 0.4% of Estimated Total Recurrent
Revenue.
Passed in Parliament this 20th day of December in the year of our Lord two
thousand and Twenty Four.
PARAN UMAR TARAWALLY,
Clerk of Parliament.
Tus Prive Impression has been carefully compared by me with the Bill
which has passed Parliament and found by me to be a true and correct printed copy
of the said Bill.
PARAN UMAR TARAWALLY,
Clerk of Parliament.
